word_list.pronunciation: stˈoʊlən kˈɪsᵻz ɑːɹ ˈɔːlweɪz ðə swˈiːɾəst

senses.definition: used to suggest that there is an added sense of excitement and pleasure associated with doing something that is forbidden or taboo, particularly in romantic encounters

word_list.word: an open door may tempt a saint
word_list.phonetic_text: æn oʊ.pən dɔ:r meɪ tɛmpt ə seɪnt
word_list.phonetic_am_text: ān ooo.pēn dawr mei tempt ē seint
word_list.pronunciation: ɐn ˈoʊpən dˈoːɹ mˈeɪ tˈɛmpt ɐ sˈeɪnt

senses.definition: used to suggest that even individuals with strong moral values and principles may be susceptible to temptation and that it is important to be mindful of one's actions and choices
used to emphasize the importance of staying engaged and productive in order to avoid negative or harmful behaviors or habits

word_list.word: the hole calls (out to|) the thief
word_list.pronunciation: ðə hˈoʊl kˈɔːlz ˈaʊt tʊ ðə θˈiːf

senses.definition: used to suggest that an exposed weakness or vulnerability can invite or tempt individuals with malicious intent to take advantage of the situation
used to suggest that people who are presented with the chance to steal or commit a crime are more likely to do so, even if they would not ordinarily engage in such behavior

word_list.word: a moment on the lips, forever on the hips
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ mˈoʊmənt ɑːnðə lˈɪps fɚɹˈɛvɚɹ ɑːnðə hˈɪps

senses.definition: used to suggest that the pleasure of indulging in unhealthy food is only temporary, but the negative effects on our body can be long-lasting and difficult to reverse

word_list.word: away goes (the devil|evil) when he finds the door shut against him
word_list.pronunciation: ɐwˈeɪ ɡoʊz ðə dˈɛvəl ɔːɹ ˈiːvəl wɛn hiː fˈaɪndz ðə dˈoːɹ ʃˈʌt ɐɡˈɛnst hˌɪm

senses.definition: used to suggest that by resisting negative influences and temptations, one can prevent them from causing harm or leading to negative consequences

word_list.word: the forbidden fruit is the sweetest
word_list.pronunciation: ðə fəbˈɪdən fɹˈuːt ɪz ðə swˈiːɾəst

senses.definition: used to suggest that things that are forbidden or unattainable often appear more desirable and tempting

examples.x: After being told not to read a particular book, Susan found herself more curious about it, as the forbidden fruit is the sweetest.

word_list.word: resist the devil, and he will flee from you
word_list.pronunciation: ɹɪsˈɪst ðə dˈɛvəl ænd hiː wɪl flˈiː fɹʌm juː

senses.definition: used to encourage individuals to exercise self-control and discipline in order to resist temptations and negative influences that may lead to harmful actions or behaviors

word_list.word: wisdom will save you also from the adulterous woman, from the wayward woman with her seductive words
word_list.pronunciation: wˈɪsdəm wɪl sˈeɪv juː ˈɑːlsoʊ fɹʌmðɪ ɐdˈʌltɚɹəs wˈʊmən fɹʌmðə wˈeɪwɚd wˈʊmən wɪð hɜː sɪdˈʌktɪv wˈɜːdz

senses.definition: used to suggest that wisdom can protect a person from the tempting and dishonest words of a woman who is often not faithful
